key_points: Geometric construction is the process of drawing shapes using only a ruler and a compass
A ruler is used to draw straight lines, while a compass is used to draw arcs and circles
Parallel lines are lines in a plane that never meet, no matter how far they are extended
Tilings (tessellations) are patterns of shapes that cover a surface without gaps or overlaps
examples: Tools: Using a 15 cm ruler and a metallic compass
Parallel lines: The opposite edges of a ruler or a railway track
Simple Tiling: Square floor tiles in a kitchen
Base line: Drawing a 5 cm segment as the start of a triangle
formulas: Parallel line property $ightarrow$ Distance between two parallel lines remains constant
Sum of angles at a point in tiling $ightarrow 360^{\circ}$
exam_tips: Keep your pencil tip sharp for precise arcs and points
Always draw a rough sketch before starting the actual construction
Ensure the compass hinge is tight so the radius doesn't change mid-draw
Identify the given measurements (sides and angles) clearly before beginning
